entries.reason: Single Responsibility — The draft stays focused on one concern: removing `approach` and integrating intent confirmation into draft start.; Unambiguous Requirements — Several statements use non-verifiable wording (e.g., “認知負荷を下げる”, “儀式感”, “十分”) instead of measurable conditions.; Complete Context — Many requirement bullets are change directives without explicit trigger-condition + expected behavior (“When/If ... shall ...”) structure.; No Hardcoded Secrets — No API keys, passwords, tokens, or other secrets are present in the draft content.; Prioritize Requirements — There are more than three requirements, but no explicit priority ordering is provided.; Impact on Existing Features — A dedicated section explicitly lists impacts on `flow.json`, `flow-auto`, `flow-status`, descriptions, and observability.; Present Recommendations with Reasoning — Each Q&A item provides a chosen answer and brief basis tied to constraints/code-flow implications.; Explore Before Asking — The draft shows issues were resolved via code/flow analysis and documented decisions rather than leaving obvious exploratory questions open.; Proactively Raise Related Implications — It proactively calls out compatibility breakage, migration need, test updates, observability checks, and future extensibility.; Confirm Brainstorm vs. Decision Before Critiquing — Inapplicable for this finalized draft artifact; it records agreed decisions and explicit user approval.; Capture Digressions in draft.md — Digressions and decision history are captured via Q&A and “Alternatives Considered” within the draft.; Draft Stays at Requirements Level — The draft includes implementation-level specifics (file paths, command/flag plumbing, internal schema, function references).; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — It notes incompatibility and manual migration need but does not provide a concrete migration plan for changed/removed behavior.

Single Responsibility — The draft stays focused on one concern: removing `approach` and integrating intent confirmation into draft flow.; Unambiguous Requirements — Most requirements use concrete `When/If ... shall ...` conditions with testable outcomes.; Complete Context — Some requirements (e.g., R9, R10) lack explicit trigger context (`When/If`) and are incomplete.; No Hardcoded Secrets — No API keys, passwords, tokens, or similar secrets appear in the content.; Prioritize Requirements — Requirements are explicitly prioritized as P1, P2, and P3.; Impact on Existing Features — Impact is explicitly described across flow model, skills, tests, and migration/compatibility notes.; Present Recommendations with Reasoning — Q&A gives recommendations, but rationale basis is not consistently tied to docs/, guardrails, or code patterns per question.; Explore Before Asking — The draft reflects prior investigation of existing flow/skills behavior rather than deferring to user questions.; Proactively Raise Related Implications — It proactively covers migration, compatibility, and downstream user-environment implications.; Confirm Brainstorm vs. Decision Before Critiquing — The draft does not explicitly confirm brainstorming vs decision mode before evaluative discussion.; Capture Digressions in draft.md — Side discussions are captured in `Q&A` and `Alternatives Considered`.; Draft Stays at Requirements Level — It includes implementation-level specifics (internal command sequencing, metric increment details, concrete operational steps).; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — CLI changes are accompanied by explicit compatibility notes and a migration plan for non-compatible state data.
line 126: unchecked task/question (- [ ] なし（draft 段階で全項目解決済み）)

Single Responsibility — すべて `approach` フェーズ廃止とその波及（core/skill/test）に集中している。; Unambiguous Requirements — R12 の「新しい対象範囲を表す文言」は判定基準が曖昧で検証不能。; Complete Context — 要件は各項目で `When/If` と `shall` の期待動作が対になっている。; No Hardcoded Secrets — 仕様文書内に秘密情報の直書きはなく、この観点は実質的に非該当。; No Silent Error Swallowing — 空 `catch` やエラー握り潰しの指示はなく、この観点は非該当。; Prioritize Requirements — 要件が P1/P2/P3 で優先度順に明示されている。; Impact on Existing Features — Migration Plan と CLI 互換性節で既存挙動への影響を明記している。; Bounded Resource Usage — エッジケースの「再質問ループ」に上限回数などの明示的境界がない。; Changes Require Test Coverage — ユニット/e2e/軽量検証スクリプトまで含めてテスト追加・更新が定義されている。; Spec Includes Test Strategy — `Test Strategy` セクションで何をどう検証するか具体化されている。; Synthesize Draft, Do Not Copy — ドラフト内容を要約再構成しており、与えられた内容外の創作も見当たらない。; Record Unresolved Points as Open Questions — `Open Questions` セクションがあり、未解決なしを明示している。; Include 'Why This Approach' Rationale — `Why This Approach` で採用理由が複数観点で説明されている。; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — 互換性影響と移行手順（Migration Plan）が明示されている。; Exit Code Contract — 失敗時に 0 を返す設計記述はなく、成功時 `npm test` が 0 であることのみを要求している。

Single Responsibility — `approach` 廃止と draft Q1 統合という単一テーマに収束している。; Unambiguous Requirements — 要件は `When/If ... shall ...` 形式で、検証可能な条件が明示されている。; Complete Context — R1〜R12 の各要件にトリガー条件と期待動作が対で含まれている。; No Hardcoded Secrets — 本文は仕様記述のみで、APIキー等の秘密情報は含まれていない。; No Silent Error Swallowing — 例外を握りつぶす実装記述はなく、この内容では本条項は実質的に非該当。; Prioritize Requirements — 要件が P1/P2/P3 で優先度順に整理されている。; Impact on Existing Features — `Migration Plan` と `CLI 表層の互換性` で既存機能への影響が明示されている。; Bounded Resource Usage — 再質問ループに「上限 = 1回」が明記され、無制限処理を避けている。; Changes Require Test Coverage — ユニット/e2e/軽量検証スクリプトまで含むテスト方針が定義されている。; Spec Includes Test Strategy — `Test Strategy` セクションで何をどう検証するかが具体化されている。; Synthesize Draft, Do Not Copy — 構造化された仕様形式で整理されており、単純な草案貼り付けには見えない。; Record Unresolved Points as Open Questions — `Open Questions` に「なし（全項目解決済み）」と明記されている。; Include 'Why This Approach' Rationale — `Why This Approach` セクションで採用理由が示されている。; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — 互換性の扱いと破壊的変更時の移行手順が `Migration Plan` に記載されている。; Exit Code Contract — 失敗時に非0終了コードを返す契約が仕様内で明示されていない。

R2 `flow get status` (new — model flows) excludes `approach` in `steps` — status derives from flow steps/state now defined without `approach`.; R3 `flow set init — -issue/--request` saves values to preparing-flow state — `set-init` parses both options and passes them to `createPreparingFlow(...)`.; R4 `flow prepare` inherits preparing `issue/request` only when CLI args are omitted — `run-prepare-spec` loads preparing data for `--run-id` and applies fallback only on empty CLI values.; R5 `PHASE_MAP` has no `approach` key — `approach` entry was removed from `PHASE_MAP`.; R6 flow — plan question order is work-env → base-branch → draft Q1 — SKILL sequence was rewritten to that order.; R7 draft Q1 uses preparing/issue summary with `[1][2][3]` choice format — flow-plan SKILL explicitly instructs summary from issue/request and exact choice confirmation.; R8 after draft Q1 `[1]`, skill internally runs `flow prepare` before continuing draft — flow-plan SKILL defines prepare-spec as silent internal step after Q1 approval.; R9 flow — auto resume judgment excludes `approach` — `sdd-forge.flow-auto` plan-phase list removed `approach`.; R10 flow — status templates/examples exclude `approach` — `sdd-forge.flow-status` step example was updated to start from `branch`.; R11 `npm test` passes all existing unit/e2e under new FLOW_STEPS — provided test evidence (`{"acceptance":1}`) does not show full `npm test` pass.; R12 flow — plan `description` does not contain `approach` — description text changed to “Covers draft through test phases.”; R13 invalid `flow set init — -issue` fails with non-zero and `ok:false` envelope — explicit positive-integer validation was added and now throws on invalid input.; R14 `flow prepare` fails with non — zero and `ok:false` when preparing-flow load fails — missing preparing state now throws explicit error during `--run-id` load path.
